title: Token-Based Authentication Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the world of token-based authentication and stateless security.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of token-based authentication compared to session-based?
options: It's more secure by default.
It is stateless and can be easily scaled across multiple servers.
It's the only way to log in with multiple user accounts.
It automatically generates a frontend UI for login.

questions.question: What happens after a user provides correct credentials in a token-based system?
options: The server creates a record in its session database.
The server generates a signed digital token and sends it back to the client.
The server tells every other server about the successful login.
Nothing, it's automatic.

questions.question: Which HTTP header is typically used to send the token back to the server in subsequent requests?
options: Cookie
Authorization
X-User-ID
Auth-Token

questions.question: What ensures that a token hasn't been tampered with or modified by the client?
options: The token is encrypted with a secret key.
The server keeps a copy of every token it issues.
The token includes a digital signature that can be verified by the server.
The token is only valid on a specific device.

questions.question: Which part of a token contains the actual data about the user (like userId or role)?
options: Header
Payload
Signature
None of the above.
